summary: A solid soundboard capture of a strong second set with a smoking Playing > Uncle John’s Band jam and a Baby Blue encore, though the mix is a bit hissy and guitar‑heavy.
review: The recording delivers a good‑quality SBD of the March‑18‑1991 second set, highlighted by an inspired transition from Playing in the Band into Uncle John's Band and a satisfying Baby Blue encore. While the audio isn’t pristine – it’s slightly hissy with heavy snare and guitars that sit forward in the mix – the performance is generally strong, despite a few flubs on Terrapin.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently positive comments about the musical performance (70% weight) and decent but not pristine soundboard quality (30% weight) yield an overall rating around four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Slight hiss, heavy snare and guitars sit forward; Vince’s keys are distant and Hornsby barely audible, but overall the SBD is adequate and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set flows well, with a strong post‑drums segment and a seamless jam from Playing into Uncle John’s Band; the encore rounds it out nicely.

band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's keyboards are distant and Hornsby is barely audible in this mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Heavy snare and drums are prominent; the post‑drums section received praise.
